title: SPIKE Supersedes Novel Pattern — substrate-beyond-spec uses existing primitive
statement: When a deliverable's execution surfaces substrate exceeding its declared scope, the chat-layer MUST use the SPIKE primitive (memory #17): branch off main, assess substrate, produce assessment (not a PR), terminate via one of three named outcomes — collapse-and-resume, delete-and-fu, or delete-and-new-deliverable. Authoring a novel pattern shape ('Wave 1.5', 'size A/B/C', 'sub-deliverable') as a substitute for SPIKE is forbidden. The three SPIKE terminals are the system's complete answer set for substrate-beyond-spec; nothing is missing.
rationale: Novel pattern proliferation degrades the operating discipline by multiplying the primitives the chat layer must reason about. SPIKE was designed precisely for the substrate-beyond-spec case; its three terminals cover every legitimate outcome. When chat-layer agents invent 'Wave 1.5' or 'size A/B/C' sub-shapes, they are not solving a new problem — they are solving a known problem with a forgotten primitive. The correct correction is to use SPIKE, not to extend the pattern catalog.

authority.author: EB-agent (chat), L1; relayed via schema-registry chat (Claude L1)

human-loop.gate: Chat layer recognizes substrate-beyond-spec at the moment of divergence (during dispatch authoring or mid-execution). Response: propose SPIKE with named terminal candidates, not novel pattern shape. Erik (L4) authorizes SPIKE branch; chat does not.
human-loop.automationPath: null by nature — recognition of substrate-beyond-spec is judgment-class. The chat layer must reason about whether the current execution's substrate exceeds the spec's declared scope; no mechanical check substitutes for that reasoning. This rule's terminalTier is correctly human-loop, not stalled-en-route-to-automated.

refine.action: Branch off main, edit this rule instance, validate, PR. Refinement primarily affects rationale + notes; statement itself is stable per memory #17.

transitions.trigger: SPIKE primitive established in DLP-STRATEGY 2026-03-14 (memory #17); operated as working-model practice across multiple sessions before this instance authored.

transitions.evidenceClass: Memory rule #17 captures SPIKE primitive precisely (three named terminals: collapse-and-resume, delete-and-fu, delete-and-new-deliverable). Working-model proof: SPIKE used successfully across multiple foundation-arc sessions. Failure-mode proof: prior chat-layer agent invented 'Wave 1.5' and 'size A/B/C' sub-shapes when SPIKE already covered the case; failure was recognizable at proposal-time as not-using-SPIKE; rule formalized after that failure to make recognition mechanical for future agents.
transitions.trigger: Rule promoted to formalized lifecycleStatus during d-2026-06-01-rule-pipeline-first-instances; backfilled transition since rule operated as formalized-in-practice before being schematized.

notes: Originated via EB-agent contribution (Evidence #3). Origin incident: prior chat-layer agent invented 'Wave 1.5' and 'size A/B/C' sub-shapes when substrate exceeded spec; SPIKE already covered the case. Capstone instance for D-3: proves rule.schema.json's terminal-by-nature property — terminalTier: human-loop is a COMPLETE state for judgment rules, not a stalled climb. The schema's automationPath field declaring 'null by nature' explicitly captures the design decision. Without this instance, the schema's claim about human-loop-as-complete-terminal is unproven. Credit: EB-agent for rule shape.
